records.ety: [L. disseminatus, p. p. of disseminare to disseminate; dis- + seminare to sow, semen seed. See Seminary.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disseminated; p. pr. & vb. n. Disseminating.]

defs: to spread around widely; to sow broadcast or as seed; to scatter for growth and propagation, like seed; to spread abroad; to diffuse; as, principles, ideas, opinions, and errors are disseminated when they are spread abroad for propagation.
To spread or extend by dispersion.
quotes: A nearly uniform and constant fire or heat disseminated throughout the body of the earth.
